title: Smoky Fermented French Tofu Casserole
description: A creamy, indulgent dish that combines the earthy depth of smoked paprika with the tangy richness of fermented miso, all baked to caramelized perfection and dairyfree friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, miso paste, smoked paprika, maple syrup, salt, pepper, and thyme until smooth.
Add the diced tofu, onions, and minced garlic to the bowl and gently toss to coat all ingredients evenly.
Transfer the mixture to a greased 8‑inch square baking dish, spreading it out in a single layer.
Pour the vegetable stock over the tofu, making sure it is evenly distributed.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for another 10 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly.
Let the casserole rest for 5 minutes before serving so the flavors meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh herbs or a sprinkle of additional nutritional yeast.
Enjoy a creamy, smoky, fermented French tofu experience that’s both indulgent and dairy‑free.
